title: podman docker socket works differently when journald is set to MaxLevelStore=Warning
body:
### Issue Description

This is a follow up on a Woodpecker CI Bug Report that i am not sure where its caused but whatever causes it: its cursed in some way.

I am running a CI Container (Woodpecker) as a non-root user with access to the user podman socket (podman-docker installed). This works fine in the default setup - containers start, stop and send their output to the ci server just fine.

However when 
```
/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/10-journald.conf
MaxLevelStore=warning
```

everything stops working. Containers are never detected as "finished" and their logs, which do exist, are only sent when cancelling the process from cli or from the woodpecker gui.

For Reference:
https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker/issues/2035

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set up a new user and podman-docker with systemctl --user enable podman.socket
2. Start woodpecker ci with access to the user socket
3. Limit journald to MaxLeveLStore=warning


### Describe the results you received

Woodpecker never gets notified of containers finishing their task

### Describe the results you expected

The journald log level should have no difference in how podman works, especially when container logs are set to "json" format.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
>   eventLogger: journald

Juts a guess but if we use journald for events it will likely not have any events in the log and many external tool rely on these events to detect the container state changes.

So my guess is you need to change the event logger to file in containers.conf to make it work.

Can confirm that it works with 

```
~/.config/containers/containers.conf 
[containers]
log_driver="json-file"

[engine]
events_logger="file"
```
